chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. D – 82 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.993/2023 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For orders on MA No.994/2023 (Ex./A) 4. For hearing of bail application 17.10.2023 Mr. Arif Ali Lashari, Advocate for applicants/accused..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4. Through this bail application, applicants/accused (i) Ali Raza S/o Ali Hassan, (ii) Sain Dino S/o Mir Muhammad alias Miran, (iii) Sadiq Ali S/o Khadim Ali and (iv) Ahsan Ali S/o Mukhtiar Ali, all by caste Katohar, seek protective bail in Crime No.278 of 2014, registered at Police Station ‘A’ Section, Khairpur under Sections 324, 295-A, 298-A, 427, 337-A(i), 337-F(i), 337-H, 147, 148, 149 PPC and 7 of Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997. Learned Counsel contends that applicants are innocent, have been falsely implicated in this case. He further contends that applicants are ready to appear before the Court concerned along with an application under Section 498 CrPC to prove their innocence, however, they apprehend their immediate arrest as non-bailable warrants have been issued against them. Hence, they seek protective bail. Cr. Bail App. No. D – 82 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 Without touching merits of the case, protective bail for a period of five days w.e.f. 17.10.2023 to 21.10.2023 (both days inclusive) is granted to applicants subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court.

No. D – 82 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 Without touching merits of the case, protective bail for a period of five days w.e.f. 17.10.2023 to 21.10.2023 (both days inclusive) is granted to applicants subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Operation of non-bailable warrants in this case shall remain suspended during aforesaid period, in which applicants are directed to surrender before the Court concerned. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of above mentioned period or whenever applicants surrender before the Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is, however, made clear that in case applicants fail to surrender before the Court concerned within stipulated period, their bail bonds shall stand automatically forfeited. Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
